question: Is the 'online Costco' private-label model viable for PDD, and how does it compare to precedents?

findings.claim: PDD officially launched 'Xinpinmu' self-operated brand initiative on March 25, 2026, with RMB 15B initial injection and RMB 100B planned over 3 years
findings.data: PDD Holdings announced formation of 'Xinpinmu' (New Pinmo), a new entity in Shanghai with RMB 15 billion initial cash injection. Over three years, PDD plans to invest RMB 100 billion ($14.5B) total to build self-operated brands integrating Pinduoduo + Temu supply chain resources, targeting both domestic and global markets across multiple categories.

findings.claim: PDD has RMB 422.3B cash reserves to fund the initiative, with FY2025 net profit of RMB 99.3B despite 12% profit decline
findings.data: As of December 31, 2025, PDD held RMB 422.3 billion in cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments (up from RMB 331.6B at end of 2024). FY2025 revenue was RMB 431.8B (+10% YoY), non-GAAP net profit RMB 107.3B (-12% YoY). PDD surpassed Alibaba for the first time as the most profitable Chinese e-commerce company.

findings.claim: Xinpinmu represents a strategic shift from pure platform to supply chain owner, announced as part of 'recreate a PDD in 3 years' vision
findings.data: At December 2025 shareholders' meeting, Co-Chairman Zhao Jiazhen announced 'recreate a Pinduoduo in 3 years' strategy. Xinpinmu is the first major execution step. The model involves PDD becoming the 'principal' (acting as buyer/brand owner) rather than just a marketplace connecting buyers and sellers. Three pillars: (1) form Xinpinmu entity investing RMB 100B, (2) deploy teams to industrial clusters with digital/manufacturing solutions, (3) launch comprehensive go-global brand services (product standards, warehousing, IP, legal, regulatory).

findings.claim: PDD's prior self-operated venture 'Fulishe' failed within 4 hours due to merchant backlash in 2023
findings.data: PDD's previous self-operated venture 'Fulishe' (welfare shop) in 2023 faced severe merchant backlash. Merchants flooded the platform with complaints and coordinated 'refund-only' campaigns, forcing the store to close within 4 hours. This demonstrates significant merchant hostility toward platform self-competition.

findings.claim: PDD's current 23% net margin derives from light-asset model; self-operated retail fundamentally changes this economics
findings.data: PDD's current profitability (23.02% net margin) derives from light-asset operations: collecting merchant fees and advertising revenue without inventory costs. Self-operated retail fundamentally changes this economics while threatening existing merchant relationships. If Xinpinmu succeeds, it could cannibalize existing merchant revenue streams.
